input.command:
git add src/tabs/Settings.tsx src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx tests/import-export.spec.ts tests/import.spec.ts tests/workouts.spec.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
settings: move Export / Import Backup from Workouts toolbar into Settings

The Workouts toolbar mixed day-navigation (Back / Advance) with data
controls (Export / Import Backup / Reset), forcing the data buttons to
be always-visible at the bottom of the workout view. The data controls
belong with other configuration in Settings.

This commit adds a new Data section to Settings hosting Export and
Import Backup, and removes those controls from the Workouts toolbar.
The program-import flow (empty-state CTA) is unchanged.

Architecture note: App.tsx conditionally mounts each tab, so when the
user switches back to Workouts after a backup import in Settings, the
Workouts effect re-runs loadState / loadLog / loadProgram and picks up
the fresh data from IDB. No cross-tab event system needed — Settings
just writes through the existing exportImport helpers.

Reset stays in the Workouts toolbar for now; it's destructive and
groups naturally with backup data so it may also belong in Settings,
left as a separate decision.
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --reviewer patflynn --title "settings: move Export / Import Backup from Workouts toolbar into Settings" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Move the **Export** and **Import Backup** controls from the bottom of the Workouts tab into a new **Data** section in the Settings tab. They're configuration/data controls and group naturally with the existing Appearance and LLM provider sections.

- New `Data` card in Settings holds Export + Import Backup (with the import message display).
- Workouts toolbar now contains only day-navigation (Back / Advance) and Reset.
- Test IDs (`export-btn`, `import-backup-label`, `import-input`, `import-message`) are preserved so the existing assertions still work — tests just navigate to Settings first.
- The empty-state program-import flow (`import-program-btn` / `import-program-input`) is unchanged; it's program-specific and stays in Workouts.

## Architecture note

`App.tsx` conditionally renders each tab (`{active === 'workouts' && <Workouts />}`), so when the user switches back to Workouts after importing a backup in Settings, Workouts re-mounts and its initial effect re-runs `loadState() + loadLog() + loadProgram()` against IDB — picking up the freshly imported data. Settings only needs to write through the existing `exportImport` helpers; no cross-tab event system or state lift required.

## Open question for reviewer

**Reset is still in the Workouts toolbar — should it also move to Settings under Data?** It's destructive and groups naturally with backup data. Left for a separate decision so this PR stays focused on the Export / Import move.

## Manual sanity check

- Workouts bottom toolbar now shows only `← Back`, `Complete & Advance →`, and `Reset`.
- Settings shows three cards: Appearance, LLM provider, Data. The Data card contains Export and Import Backup, with the import status message appearing below them after an import.

## Test plan
- [x] `nix develop --command npm run typecheck` — passes
- [x] `nix develop --command bash -c "npm ci && npm run build"` — passes
- [x] `nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test --project=chromium` — 13/13 pass, including the relocated import/export assertions
- [x] `klaus _pre-review` — no issues

Run: 20260518-2057-bf8abd79
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/gunk-dev/flux/pull/76

**Summary of changes:**
- Added a new **Data** card to `src/tabs/Settings.tsx` with Export + Import Backup, including its own `importMessage` state and display.
- Removed `handleExport`, `handleImport`, the toolbar export/import-backup controls, and unused `exportImport` imports from `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx`. The Workouts toolbar now has just Back / Advance / Reset.
- The `importMessage` state and display remain in Workouts for the program-import flow (out of scope).
- Updated 3 test files to navigate to Settings before triggering backup import/export, then back to Workouts when verifying workout-tab state. All 13 Playwright tests pass.
- Reset was intentionally left in the Workouts toolbar; called out as a follow-up question in the PR body.

**Summary of changes:**
- Added a new **Data** card to `src/tabs/Settings.tsx` with Export + Import Backup, including its own `importMessage` state and display.
- Removed `handleExport`, `handleImport`, the toolbar export/import-backup controls, and unused `exportImport` imports from `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx`. The Workouts toolbar now has just Back / Advance / Reset.
- The `importMessage` state and display remain in Workouts for the program-import flow (out of scope).
- Updated 3 test files to navigate to Settings before triggering backup import/export, then back to Workouts when verifying workout-tab state. All 13 Playwright tests pass.
- Reset was intentionally left in the Workouts toolbar; called out as a follow-up question in the PR body.
